query: Does vitamin D supplementation reduce the risk of respiratory infections?

contradiction.description: A supplement page claims a far larger effect than the meta-analyses.

sources.title: Vitamin D for preventing acute respiratory infections: a systematic review and meta-analysis
sources.snippet: Supplementation reduced the risk of acute respiratory infection by about 12 percent overall, with a larger effect in deficient individuals.

sources.title: Vitamin D and respiratory tract infections: meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials
sources.snippet: Pooled data from randomized controlled trials show roughly an 11 percent reduction in respiratory infections.

sources.title: Vitamin D and immunity
sources.snippet: Some studies suggest a modest 10 percent benefit, especially in people who are deficient.

sources.title: Vitamin D slashes infections by 50%
sources.snippet: High-dose vitamin D can cut your risk of respiratory infection by 50 percent or more.

query: Does vitamin C prevent the common cold?

contradiction.description: Supplement marketing claims prevention the trials do not support.

sources.title: Vitamin C for preventing and treating the common cold: systematic review
sources.snippet: Regular supplementation did not reduce cold incidence in the general population, about a 3 percent change, though duration was modestly shorter.

sources.title: Vitamin C fact sheet for health professionals
sources.snippet: Routine vitamin C does not appear to prevent colds in most people; effects on duration are small, around 8 percent.

sources.title: Vitamin C prevents and cures colds
sources.snippet: Megadoses of vitamin C prevent colds in 90 percent of people.

query: Is intermittent fasting effective for type 2 diabetes management?

sources.title: Intermittent fasting and glycemic control in type 2 diabetes: a randomized controlled trial
sources.snippet: Time-restricted eating improved HbA1c modestly over 6 months in a randomized controlled trial.

sources.title: Type 2 diabetes: dietary management guidance
sources.snippet: Evidence is mixed; intermittent fasting may help some patients but is not recommended universally.

sources.title: Fasting reverses diabetes
sources.snippet: Intermittent fasting can reverse type 2 diabetes for almost everyone.

query: Do statins reduce cardiovascular events in primary prevention?

sources.title: Statins for the primary prevention of cardiovascular disease: systematic review
sources.snippet: Statins reduced major vascular events by about 25 percent per mmol/L LDL reduction.

sources.title: Efficacy and safety of statin therapy: meta-analysis
sources.snippet: Each 1 mmol/L LDL reduction lowered major vascular events by roughly 22 percent.

query: Do omega-3 supplements prevent cardiovascular disease?

sources.title: Omega-3 fatty acids for cardiovascular disease prevention: systematic review
sources.snippet: Supplementation had little or no effect on cardiovascular events overall, about a 2 percent change.

sources.title: Marine n-3 fatty acids and cardiovascular outcomes: a randomized trial
sources.snippet: No significant reduction in major cardiovascular events versus placebo.

sources.title: Omega-3 and heart health
sources.snippet: Fish oil may modestly help triglycerides; effects on events are uncertain.

query: Does creatine supplementation improve cognitive performance?

sources.title: Creatine and cognition: evidence review
sources.snippet: A meta-analysis suggests a small benefit to short-term memory, strongest under sleep deprivation.

sources.title: Creatine and brain function
sources.snippet: Evidence is preliminary; some randomized trials show modest cognitive effects.

query: At what age should breast cancer screening with mammography begin?

sources.title: Breast cancer screening guidance
sources.snippet: Routine mammography is recommended from age 50, with individualized discussion earlier.

sources.title: Screening for breast cancer with mammography: systematic review
sources.snippet: Benefits and harms are finely balanced; absolute mortality benefit is modest.

query: Does reducing dietary salt lower blood pressure?

sources.title: Reduced dietary salt and blood pressure: systematic review and meta-analysis
sources.snippet: Salt reduction lowered systolic blood pressure by roughly 4 mmHg on average.

sources.title: Salt reduction
sources.snippet: Lowering salt intake reduces blood pressure and cardiovascular risk at the population level.

query: Are probiotics effective for irritable bowel syndrome?

sources.title: Probiotics for irritable bowel syndrome: systematic review
sources.snippet: Some strains may improve symptoms, but evidence quality is low to moderate.

sources.title: Management of irritable bowel syndrome
sources.snippet: Probiotics show a small benefit for global IBS symptoms in pooled trials.

query: Does hormone replacement therapy increase breast cancer risk?

contradiction.description: An older promotional page understates the risk later trials established.

sources.title: Type and timing of menopausal hormone therapy and breast cancer risk: meta-analysis
sources.snippet: Combined HRT increased breast cancer risk by roughly 26 percent with prolonged use.

sources.title: Menopausal hormone therapy and cancer risk
sources.snippet: Combined estrogen-progestin therapy raises breast cancer risk by about 24 percent.

sources.title: HRT is completely safe
sources.snippet: Hormone therapy carries essentially no increased cancer risk, about 1 percent.

query: Is moderate coffee consumption associated with lower mortality?

sources.title: Coffee consumption and mortality: a prospective cohort study
sources.snippet: Drinking 3 to 4 cups daily was associated with about a 12 percent lower mortality.

sources.title: Coffee and health outcomes: umbrella review
sources.snippet: Moderate intake is associated with lower all-cause mortality, around 10 percent.

query: Does vitamin E supplementation reduce mortality?

sources.title: Antioxidant supplements including vitamin E for mortality: systematic review
sources.snippet: High-dose vitamin E showed no benefit and a possible small increase in mortality.

sources.title: Vitamin E dosage and all-cause mortality: a meta-analysis
sources.snippet: High-dose supplementation was associated with a roughly 4 percent higher mortality.

query: Is exercise an effective treatment for depression?

sources.title: Exercise for depression: systematic review and meta-analysis
sources.snippet: Exercise produced a moderate reduction in depressive symptoms versus control.

sources.title: Exercise as treatment for depression: network meta-analysis
sources.snippet: Most exercise modalities reduced depression, with effects comparable to some therapies.

query: Does zinc shorten the duration of the common cold?

sources.title: Zinc for the common cold: systematic review
sources.snippet: Zinc lozenges started early may shorten cold duration by roughly 2 days, evidence quality varies.

sources.title: Zinc fact sheet
sources.snippet: Some trials suggest zinc may modestly reduce cold duration, by about 1 to 2 days.

query: Is a low-carb diet more effective than low-fat for weight loss?

contradiction.description: A diet-marketing page claims a dramatic advantage trials did not find.

sources.title: Low-fat vs low-carbohydrate diet for weight loss: a randomized trial
sources.snippet: At 12 months the difference in weight loss was small, about 1 kg, and not significant.

sources.title: Comparison of weight-loss diets: a randomized trial
sources.snippet: Adherence mattered more than macronutrient mix; differences were roughly 2 kg.

sources.title: Keto melts fat 5x faster
sources.snippet: Low-carb dieters lost 25 kg, far more than low-fat dieters.
